title: Implement interpreter execution loop and foundational opcodes
purpose: This is the engine that makes everything else work. The execution loop fetches instructions, dispatches them, counts reductions, and yields when the budget runs out. The foundational opcodes — move, call, return, allocate, deallocate, test_heap, put_list, put_tuple2, get_tuple_element — are the minimum set needed to execute any Gleam function. Without this brief, we have data structures and registries but nothing that runs. Every subsequent interpreter brief (B-013 through B-016) builds on these primitives.
task: Implement the core interpreter in crates/beamr/src/interpreter/. The execution loop (mod.rs) fetches the instruction at the current code pointer, dispatches it via the opcode table, and advances the pointer. Reduction counting: decrement on every call/apply, yield when zero. Implement the foundational opcodes in opcodes/core.rs: label/func_info (metadata), move (register/literal/stack transfer), call/call_only/call_ext/call_ext_only/call_last/call_ext_last (function calls + tail calls), return, allocate/allocate_zero/deallocate (stack frame management), test_heap (heap space check), put_list (cons cell), put_tuple2 (tuple), get_tuple_element (element access).

requirements.title: Execution loop
requirements.spec: beamr::interpreter SHALL implement a run(process, module) -> ExecutionResult function that executes instructions in a loop. Each iteration SHALL: read the instruction at the current code pointer, dispatch it to the appropriate handler, and advance the pointer (or jump). ExecutionResult SHALL be one of: Yielded (reduction budget exhausted), Waiting (blocked on receive), Exited(reason). It SHALL NOT return control to the caller mid-instruction — each instruction completes atomically.
acceptance: run() with a single 'return' instruction returns Exited(Normal)
run() with a call chain executes all instructions in sequence
run() with a tight loop yields after the reduction budget is exhausted
ExecutionResult has variants: Yielded, Waiting, Exited(ExitReason)

requirements.title: Opcode dispatch directory structure
requirements.spec: beamr::interpreter::opcodes SHALL be a directory module with: mod.rs (dispatch table mapping opcode numbers to handler functions), core.rs (foundational opcodes from this brief). Future briefs add guards.rs, messaging.rs, closures.rs, binary.rs. The dispatch table SHALL map each opcode to its handler. Unknown opcodes SHALL return an ExecError. It SHALL NOT exceed 500 lines per file.
acceptance: crates/beamr/src/interpreter/opcodes/ is a directory with mod.rs and core.rs
opcodes/mod.rs contains the dispatch table and re-exports
opcodes/core.rs contains all foundational opcode handlers
Unknown opcodes produce ExecError with the opcode number

requirements.title: Reduction counting and preemptive yield
requirements.spec: WHEN the interpreter executes a call or apply instruction THE SYSTEM SHALL decrement the process's reduction counter. WHEN the counter reaches zero THE SYSTEM SHALL save the process state (code pointer, registers) and return Yielded. The scheduler resets the budget and re-schedules. It SHALL NOT skip the decrement on any call variant — every function entry point costs a reduction.
acceptance: A call instruction decrements the reduction counter by 1
A process with budget 4000 yields after exactly 4000 call instructions
After yielding, the process state (registers, code pointer) is preserved
Resuming a yielded process continues from where it left off

requirements.title: Label, func_info, and move instructions
requirements.spec: label SHALL mark a jump target (no-op during sequential execution). func_info SHALL store module/function/arity for error reporting. move SHALL copy a term from source (X reg, Y reg, literal, atom) to destination (X reg, Y reg). It SHALL NOT allocate heap space — move copies the 64-bit Term value only.
acceptance: label is a no-op that does not modify registers or stack
func_info stores module, function, arity for error reporting
move from X register to X register copies the term
move from literal to X register loads the literal
move does not allocate heap space

requirements.title: Call instructions — local, external, and tail calls
requirements.spec: call SHALL save the return address and jump to a label. call_only SHALL jump without saving. call_ext/call_ext_only SHALL resolve via module or BIF registry. call_last/call_ext_last SHALL deallocate the current frame before jumping (tail call). All call variants SHALL decrement the reduction counter. It SHALL NOT leave stale return addresses after a tail call.
acceptance: call saves return address and jumps to label
call_ext resolves module:function/arity via the module registry
call_ext to a registered BIF invokes the native function
call_last deallocates N stack slots then jumps (tail call)
All call variants decrement the reduction counter

requirements.title: Return instruction
requirements.spec: WHEN the interpreter executes return THE SYSTEM SHALL pop the return address from the stack and jump to it. IF the stack is empty (initial call frame) THE SYSTEM SHALL exit the process with reason Normal.
acceptance: return pops the return address and resumes at the caller
return on the initial frame exits the process with Normal
After return, the stack frame count is decremented by one

requirements.title: Stack frame management and heap check
requirements.spec: allocate SHALL push a frame with N Y slots. allocate_zero SHALL initialize Y slots to NIL. deallocate SHALL pop the frame. test_heap SHALL verify N words available, signaling GC-needed if not. It SHALL NOT silently ignore heap exhaustion.
acceptance: allocate(3) creates a frame with 3 Y-register slots
allocate_zero(3) creates a frame with 3 slots all set to NIL
deallocate removes the top frame
test_heap(10) succeeds when 10+ words available
test_heap(10) signals GC-needed when insufficient

requirements.title: Data construction opcodes
requirements.spec: put_list SHALL allocate a cons cell on the heap from head and tail. put_tuple2 SHALL allocate a tuple from elements. get_tuple_element SHALL extract the Nth element into a register. It SHALL NOT allow get_tuple_element on non-tuple terms — raises badarg.
acceptance: put_list with head=1, tail=NIL creates [1]
put_tuple2 with [1, 2, 3] creates a 3-tuple
get_tuple_element(tuple, 0) stores the first element in the destination register
get_tuple_element on a non-tuple raises badarg

requirements.title: Gate 1 proof-of-life test
requirements.spec: WHEN the interpreter is tested THE SYSTEM SHALL include a test that loads a .beam file, spawns a process, and runs it to completion — validating the full pipeline (load → spawn → execute → exit).
acceptance: A test loads a compiled Gleam fibonacci module, calls fib(10), gets the correct result
The process exits normally after the function returns
cargo test -p beamr interpreter passes with all tests green

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement branching/guard opcodes — B-013 scope
SHALL NOT implement send/receive or exception opcodes — B-014 scope
SHALL NOT implement closure, apply, or map opcodes — B-015 scope
SHALL NOT implement binary opcodes — B-016 scope
SHALL NOT implement the scheduler — this brief produces ExecutionResult, the scheduler (B-012) drives the loop
verification: cargo test -p beamr interpreter passes with all tests green
A compiled Gleam module loads and executes to completion (Gate 1 proof-of-life)
Code inspection: every call variant decrements the reduction counter
Code inspection: opcodes/core.rs stays under 500 lines
